name: Bhai Gopal Singh (Amritsar)
body: Bhai Gopal Singh was born in Nangal Kotli, Gurdaspur, in 1929 (though some sources indicate his birth was in 1927) to Sardar Kartar Singh and Mata Jaswant Kaur. At the age of four, he lost his eyesight due to a medical mishap caused by a compounder administering the wrong medicine into his eyes. To support his education, his father arranged for him to learn Braille.

Bhai Gopal Singh’s father had a contracting business in Assam, and owned numerous properties in Gurdaspur. Unfortunately, the family faced tragedy when his parents were killed in riots prior to the partition in 1947. Following the loss of his parents, he was sent to the Central Khalsa Orphanage in Amritsar to study Gurbani and Kirtan. He was tutored on the Shabads from Sri Guru Granth Sahib, Dasam Bani, Vaaran of Bhai Gurdas Ji, and the Ghazals of Bhai Nand Lal Goya. He also trained in vocal music, harmonium, violin, tabla, and other instruments under the guidance of his Ustads, Bhai Lal Singh and Bhai Natha Singh.

Between 1953 and 1957, Bhai Dilbir Singh and Bhai Dildar Singh learned Kirtan from Bhai Gopal Singh. They later formed a jatha and accompanied him in Kirtan. From 1957 to 1970, they served as Hazoori Raagis at Gurdwara Sees Ganj Sahib in Delhi. A member of the Sangat, Sardar Mehar Singh Chadha, was so moved by Bhai Gopal Singh’s Kirtan that he advocated for his appointment as Hazoori Raagi at Sri Harmandir Sahib, Amritsar. Bhai Gopal Singh’s jatha was initially hired for one year but came under permanent employment due to their immense popularity, drawing large crowds.

Bhai Gopal Singh served in Amritsar from 1971 until 1973, when he passed away after suffering a heart attack on April 5, 1973, while visiting relatives in Delhi. He was on his way back after performing Kirtan at the annual Samagam organized by Giani Sant Singh Maskeen in Alwar, Rajasthan. His body was returned to Amritsar for cremation, and thousands from the Sangat attended his funeral.
The information above was obtained from several issues of the Amrit Kirtan magazine.
